blockers.description: The project's dev DSN (DEV_DSN, used by test fixtures) IS the same live Postgres the running docker-compose stack uses — not an isolated test DB. This caused two real data-loss incidents this session even with -m "not db_live" passed, because two test files (test_store_contract.py, test_triage_enrichment.py) had a marker helper literally named db_live that only applied pytest.mark.skipif, never the actual pytest.mark.db_live marker the -m flag filters on.
blockers.workaround: Both mislabeled helpers were fixed this session (commit 7fd0023) — -m "not db_live" now genuinely protects the live DB in those two files. The deeper architectural fix (point DEV_DSN at a genuinely separate test DB) is still not done — flagged as a real follow-up, not attempted this session (scope decision).

blockers.description: CR-01 (05-REVIEW.md): RabbitMQBus._rebuild_topology() deletes all 5 production queues (not just the mismatched one) on any AMQP 406, with no dev/prod gate. Pre-existing Phase 3 code, now live in production since Phase 5 added the first real consume() caller.
blockers.workaround: Not fixed — advisory finding from code review, does not block phase completion per GSD design. Real risk: a routine redeploy that changes any queue argument could silently drop every in-flight message in production.

human_actions_pending.context: Asked directly, no response yet before pause. Minor bug — per-channel video limits from YT_CHANNELS config are silently ignored, always falls back to default of 3.

Phase 5 fully verified and closed. Phase 6 has no CONTEXT.md yet — /gsd-discuss-phase 6 is the natural next step.

decisions.decision: Fixed both db_live-marker-naming bugs by making the helper yield (pytest.mark.db_live, pytest.mark.skipif(...)) instead of skipif alone
decisions.rationale: pytest -m "not db_live" was silently failing to deselect these fixtures' postgres-parametrized variants, causing two real data-loss/residue incidents this session despite deliberately passing that flag.

decisions.decision: Did not chase the original empty-articles silent-failure window further once ingest was confirmed working again
decisions.rationale: Not currently reproducible; the debugging skill's guidance is to document findings and move on once genuinely environmental/timing-dependent, rather than force an artificial fix. System is now healthy with 67 real articles as proof.

decisions.decision: Manually cleaned up test residue found live in production (1 stray TestSource article + its embedding row) rather than leaving it
decisions.rationale: Clearly test-fixture data (source='TestSource', url=example.com), unambiguous to remove, keeps production data clean.

next_action: Ask the user (still pending from before pause): fix youtube_ingest.py's max_per_run/max_n key mismatch now, or leave as follow-up? Then either fix it or move to Phase 6 planning (/gsd-discuss-phase 6).
context_notes: This is a between-phases pause, not mid-phase work — Phase 5 is fully closed (UAT, SECURITY, REVIEW, VERIFICATION, VALIDATION all done and committed) and Phase 6 hasn't started. The bulk of this session was: (1) resuming and closing out Phase 5's last blocking task from a prior stale handoff, (2) running the full post-Phase-5 quality gate chain (UAT/security/code-review/verification/validation) which surfaced and fixed several real bugs (LLM_BASE_URL env leak, shadow_run.py dedup-blind parity, on_message header bug redeploy), (3) a genuine data-loss incident during regression testing (misleadingly-named db_live marker in a test fixture truncated live Postgres) that was disclosed immediately and led to finding + fixing the same bug in a second file, (4) a full system health check that found and fixed a poisoned worker DB connection (37 DLQ messages), and (5) a systematic-debugging investigation into why ingest wasn't repopulating articles, which refuted the 'seen-cursor' hypothesis and left the system genuinely healthy with 67 real articles. Full docker-compose stack is up and healthy right now — nothing needs restarting on resume.
